capabilities.name: Bill of Materials (BOM)
capabilities.description: Structured definition of the components, quantities, and assembly relationships that make up a manufactured product. Foundation for every other manufacturing function.

requirements.text: Support multi-level BOM structure with parent/child/sub-assembly hierarchy to represent complex product compositions
proxy_refs: Multi-level BOM with parent/child/sub-assembly hierarchy

requirements.text: Define component quantities with unit-of-measure and scrap/waste factors for accurate material planning
proxy_refs: Component quantities with unit-of-measure and scrap factor

requirements.text: Calculate standard cost rollup from component costs to finished-good level
proxy_refs: Cost rollup from component costs to finished-good standard cost

requirements.text: Generate where-used reports to identify all BOMs and products that reference a given component
proxy_refs: Where-used report (find all BOMs that reference a given component)

requirements.text: Manage alternate and substitute components with configurable substitution rules and priority ranking
proxy_refs: Alternate / substitute components with substitution rules

requirements.text: Support phantom sub-assemblies that explode through to lower levels without creating intermediate inventory
proxy_refs: Phantom sub-assemblies (explode-through components for lean production)

requirements.text: Apply effectivity dates to control when components are added to or removed from BOMs
proxy_refs: Effectivity dates for component changes (in/out by date)

requirements.text: Distinguish between Engineering BOM and Manufacturing BOM to support design and production workflows
proxy_refs: Engineering BOM vs Manufacturing BOM distinction

requirements.text: Capture reference designators to specify component placement and location on assemblies
proxy_refs: Reference designators (which component goes where on the assembly)

features.name: BOM Structure & Hierarchy
features.description: Multi-level composition, sub-assemblies, and phantom items that define product architecture.

features.name: Component Definition
features.description: Quantities, units of measure, scrap factors, reference designators, and alternate/substitute rules.

features.name: BOM Lifecycle & Variants
features.description: Effectivity dating, engineering vs. manufacturing variants, and cost rollup calculations.

features.name: BOM Analytics & Reporting
features.description: Where-used analysis and component impact visibility across products.

capabilities.name: Routings, Operations & Work Centers

requirements.text: Work center master data includes capacity, cost rate, and efficiency metrics

requirements.text: Routing supports parallel and alternate operation paths for flexible manufacturing
proxy_refs: Parallel and alternate operation paths

requirements.text: Multiple routing versions can be maintained with effectivity dates to manage engineering changes
proxy_refs: Routing versions with effectivity dates

requirements.text: Operations link to BOM components consumed or produced at that manufacturing step

requirements.text: Work instructions and technical documentation can be attached to each operation

imda_refs.text: A source of information on the sequence of processes involved to manufacture or assemble a product (e.g. technical drawing, Bill of Material (BOM), materials required and manufacturing process such as tools, equipment, instructions used to build the product

capabilities.name: Material Requirements Planning (MRP)
capabilities.description: Net-requirements explosion from demand (sales orders + forecast) through BOMs and inventory policies, generating planned purchase and production orders to meet demand without stock-outs or excess.

requirements.text: Aggregate demand from sales orders, forecasts, and stock transfers into a consolidated demand schedule
proxy_refs: Demand aggregation from sales orders, forecasts, and stock transfers

requirements.text: Perform multi-level BOM explosion to calculate net requirements, netting against on-hand inventory and on-order quantities
proxy_refs: Multi-level BOM explosion net of on-hand and on-order quantities

requirements.text: Generate planned purchase orders for externally sourced items based on net requirements
proxy_refs: Planned order generation for purchased items and manufactured items

requirements.text: Generate planned production orders for internally manufactured items based on net requirements
proxy_refs: Planned order generation for purchased items and manufactured items

requirements.text: Offset planned order dates by supplier or manufacturing lead times to calculate required start dates
proxy_refs: Lead-time offsetting for start-date calculation

requirements.text: Apply lot-sizing rules including fixed lot, lot-for-lot, economic order quantity, and period of supply methods
proxy_refs: Lot-size rules (fixed, lot-for-lot, economic, period of supply)

requirements.text: Apply safety-stock and reorder-point policies per item to protect against demand and supply variability
proxy_refs: Safety-stock and reorder-point policies per item

requirements.text: Generate MRP exception messages identifying orders to expedite, defer, cancel, or release based on plan changes
proxy_refs: MRP exception messages (expedite, defer, cancel, release)

requirements.text: Provide visibility of dependent demand traceability from finished goods through all component levels to raw materials
proxy_refs: Dependent demand visibility from finished good to raw material

requirements.text: Support what-if scenario analysis by running MRP plans with alternative demand, supply, or policy parameters
proxy_refs: What-if MRP run for scenario testing

features.name: Planned Order Generation

features.name: Planning Analysis & Visibility
features.description: Support what-if scenario analysis and provide traceability of dependent demand across BOM levels.

capabilities.name: Production Scheduling & Capacity Planning
capabilities.description: Assign work orders to work centers across a time horizon, respecting finite capacity and operation sequence — distinct from MRP which generates orders without regard to capacity.

requirements.text: Support both infinite and finite capacity scheduling modes to allow flexible planning across different scenarios
proxy_refs: Infinite and finite capacity scheduling modes

requirements.text: Provide work center load visualization (Gantt chart or resource timeline) to display scheduled operations and available capacity
proxy_refs: Work center load view (Gantt or resource chart)

requirements.text: Generate capacity utilization reports and identify bottleneck work centers to guide capacity planning decisions
proxy_refs: Capacity utilisation reports and bottleneck identification

requirements.text: Support calendar exceptions including holidays, shift patterns, and planned downtime to reflect actual work center availability
proxy_refs: Calendar exceptions (holidays, shifts, planned downtime)

requirements.text: Enable forward scheduling from operation start date and backward scheduling from due date to optimize schedule feasibility
proxy_refs: Forward scheduling from start date, backward from due date

requirements.text: Support interactive rescheduling with drag-and-drop capability and real-time conflict detection to identify capacity violations
proxy_refs: Drag-and-drop rescheduling with conflict detection

requirements.text: Apply finite-capacity sequencing with setup-time minimization to reduce changeover delays and improve work center efficiency
proxy_refs: Finite-capacity sequencing with setup-time minimisation

requirements.text: Enable what-if scenario modeling to evaluate alternative scheduling strategies and their impact on delivery dates and resource utilization
proxy_refs: What-if scheduling scenarios

features.description: Enable drag-and-drop rescheduling, conflict detection, and setup-time optimization.

features.name: Scenario Modeling & Analysis

capabilities.name: Shop Floor Execution & MES
capabilities.description: Real-time shop-floor interface for operators: start/stop operations, record output, scan materials, capture quality, report downtime. Bridges ERP work orders (ISA-95 level 4) and the physical process (level 2).
